hints.Nominative: While 'Virgo' has the same form in nominative and vocative, here it is a direct address
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Virgo' would be 'Virginis' — 'Virgo' is the vocative form of address
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Virgo' would be 'Virginem' — 'Virgo' is vocative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'virginis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'virginis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'virginis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'Maria' here is vocative — a direct address in the prayer
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Maria' would be 'Mariae' — 'Maria' here is vocative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'Maria' would be 'Maria', but context shows direct address (vocative)

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'Mariae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Mariae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'Mariae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: While 'clemens' has the same form, the 'O' particle marks it as vocative address
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'clemens' would be 'clementis' — 'clemens' is vocative here
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'clemens' would be 'clementem' — 'clemens' is vocative

hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'dulcis' follows 'O' and is a vocative address
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'dulcis' (3rd decl. adj.) would also be 'dulcis', but the 'O' particle marks vocative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'dulcem' — 'dulcis' here is vocative

translation: O merciful, O loving, O sweet Virgin Mary
